title: lotus conjugatus subsp. requienii
intro: Lotus conjugatus subsp. requienii, also known as the Lotus Leaf or Sweet Flag, is a subspecies of the Lotus conjugatus plant, which is a type of aquatic plant that grows in marshy areas and is native to parts of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its essential oils, which have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lotus conjugatus subsp. requienii include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include essential oils, extracts, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
